name: Field activity source-work follow-up

scope.question: Do the Retro Biosciences 2022 launch and Healthspan Connect source-work holds now have enough official source support to move out of source work?

summary: Ran a bounded source-work follow-up on the two open field-activity holds. Retro Biosciences' 2022 launch remains source-held: the current Retro homepage and official blog support company mission and the already-published 2026 financing item, but targeted official-source checks did not find a durable official dated 2022 launch or $180M source; BioSpace remains secondary dated coverage. Healthspan Connect moved out of source work: ClinicalTrials.gov records NCT07209774 as a planned 100,000-participant prospective observational healthy-aging programme first posted on 2025-10-07, and a dated TwinsUK news item independently identifies Healthspan Connect as a Department of Twin Research programme with recruitment due in spring 2026. The Healthspan item should go to human approval as activity-only field infrastructure, not evidence or an interventional trial result.

materiality_decision.rationale: One watchlist item now has enough primary dated source context to enter the approval packet as activity-only field infrastructure. No source, study, finding, outlook, stage, momentum, or confidence change is warranted.

search_log.summary: Official current site confirms Retro's healthy-lifespan mission and aging-therapeutics focus, but it does not provide a dated 2022 launch or initial-funding source.
Official blog and targeted official-site searches surfaced the May 22, 2026 financing post already captured in public activity. No durable official 2022 launch page or $180M source was found.

Secondary dated coverage reports the April 6, 2022 Retro launch and $180M funding, pointing to Twitter and an older Retro page. This remains a useful lead but does not clear the default source-quality bar for publication.

Registry record lists Healthspan Connect Programme of Research as not yet recruiting, prospective observational cohort, 100,000 estimated enrollment, King's College London sponsor, estimated start 2026-01-05, estimated completion 2056-01-05, and first posted 2025-10-07.

Dated November 27, 2025 official TwinsUK item says Healthspan Connect is a new Programme of Research based at the Department of Twin Research, will lead multiple sub-studies on biological, social, and environmental factors in healthy ageing and resilience, and had recruitment due in spring 2026.

Official TwinsUK research-area page names Healthspan Connect as a related King's College London project in population-health and ageing context. This supports programme context but is not the main dated event source.

excluded_sources.title: Retro Biosciences public launch and initial funding
excluded_sources.reason: No durable official dated 2022 launch or $180M funding source was found in the bounded recheck. Keep the watchlist candidate as research_more unless a primary dated source is found or the human approves a secondary-source exception.

notes: Healthspan Connect should be treated as planned observational field infrastructure and trial-watch context, not as an interventional trial launch, result, or evidence signal.
The Retro 2022 launch is probably material, but the default publication bar remains unmet because the durable source is secondary dated coverage.
next_actions: Use the field-activity packet to ask the human whether to approve, reject, or revise the Healthspan Connect activity-only candidate.
Keep Retro Biosciences' 2022 launch as source work until a durable official dated source is found or a secondary-source exception is explicitly approved.
